stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Putin promises revenge after blaming Kyiv for Luhansk attack he says killed six
stories.summary: Ukraine dismisses Russian president’s claim, saying attack on dormitory in Russian-controlled region in the east ‘exclusively targeted the Russian war machine’. What we know on day 1,550 Russian President Vladimir Putin has blamed Ukraine for what he described as a deadly drone attack on a student dorm in Luhansk, a Russian-controlled region in eastern Ukraine, and has vowed to retaliate. Ukraine’s military denied the Russian accusations and said it had struck an elite drone command unit in the

stories.title: Tulsi Gabbard out as US intelligence chief
stories.summary: Tulsi Gabbard is stepping down as President Donald Trump’s director of national intelligence, according to a resignation letter she shared on X on Friday. In the letter, Gabbard said she was stepping aside from the role to support her husband as he battles bone cancer. She will step down from the role on June 30. Her departure comes after the U.S. launched a joint military campaign against Iran with Israel that tested her place in an unexpectedly hawkish administration.

stories.title: Russia’s drone blame game fails to split Ukraine from its Baltic allies
stories.summary: PRAGUE — Russia is trying to turn a string of stray combat drones appearing over the Baltic countries into a political crisis between Ukraine and some of its staunchest allies. So far, the effort appears to have fallen flat. After a wave of drone incidents across Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania — blamed on Russia using electronic warfare to redirect Ukrainian drones into NATO airspace — Moscow accused the three Baltic countries of allowing Ukraine to use their airspace for attacks on Russia and th

stories.title: Cancoillotte’s comeback is no surprise | Letters
stories.summary: Readers respond to an article about France’s ‘viral’ liquid cheese Yes, we French people do value taste in cheeses ( The French are hitting their protein goals – thanks to a cheese that looks like ectoplasm, 18 May ). There is a myriad of flavours in our dairy industry, from extreme farmhouse dungy-funk to sterilised stinky plastic. But most of our industrial cheeses sit firmly in the vicinity of savoury hardened fat. So it’s no surprise that cancoillotte is making a comeback! In Paris in my lat

stories.title: Friday briefing: ​As Israel’s coalition collapses, can its prime minister hang on to power?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: As ​the polls tighten a​​round Benjamin Netanyahu, the coming months may redefine Israel’s political order without resolving its most entrenched conflicts Good morning. On Wednesday, Israeli legislators took the first steps towards dissolving parliament and calling fresh nationwide elections. Leading leftwing Knesset member Yair Golan hailed it “the beginning of the end of the worst government in Israel’s history.” Benjamin Netanyahu has spent 20 of the last 30 years as Is

stories.title: ‘Ups and downs’: Czech president admits rocky relationship with PM
stories.summary: Czech President Petr Pavel admitted he had a rocky relationship with Prime Minister Andrej Babiš — but said he was willing to try and find compromises. Asked how he would describe his ties with the populist leader, he told the POLITICO Speakeasy at the GLOBSEC forum Friday: “I would say realistic, which means there are ups and downs. “We have a number of meetings and I believe we will continue to have practical communications,” the retired general said, adding jokingly, “

stories.title: Czech president urges Nato to ‘show its teeth’ over Russia’s provocations
stories.summary: Former general Petr Pavel says Moscow’s testing of alliance’s eastern flank should be met with firm line The Czech president, Petr Pavel, has urged Nato to “show its teeth” in response to Russia’s repeated testing of the alliance’s resolve on its eastern flank, suggesting a range of options including switching off its internet, cutting off its banks from global financial systems and shooting down jets that violate allied airspace. Speaking to the Guardian in Prague, Pavel called for “decisive en

stories.title: In France, pro-Palestinian solidarity is being silenced and criminalised | Rokhaya Diallo
stories.summary: The government’s deeply illiberal ‘Yadan bill’ may be dead. But the threat to legitimate protest certainly is not Tensions in France over how to respond to a rise in antisemitism have been running high. A government-backed bill that aimed to deal with the problem was rightly denounced as an attack on freedom of expression before being quietly shelved by the government last month. Introduced in 2024 by Caroline Yadan , a member of the national assembly, the draft legislation was intended to count

stories.title: Rubio tries to reassure Nato allies over US troop deployments
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says he wants to send more troops to Poland, a week after his officials cancelled a similar deployment.

stories.title: Despite ‘chaotic communication’ Polish aide says Trump is committed to troops in Europe
stories.summary: PRAGUE — A senior aide to Polish President Karol Nawrocki told POLITICO on Friday that “chaotic communication” in Washington is behind confusion over U.S. troop deployments and said the White House remains committed to boots on the ground. In an interview at the POLITICO Speakeasy at GLOBSEC, Marcin Przydacz, secretary of state to the nationalist president, said Donald Trump’s announcement late Thursday that the U.S. would send 5,000 troops to Poland was not a surprise in Warsaw. “It was very mu

stories.title: Top EU prosecutor complains to Brussels about Greece amid farm fraud probe
stories.summary: European Prosecutor Laura Codruța Kövesi sent a formal complaint to the European Commission regarding Greek moves seeking to sideline EU prosecutors from investigating lawmakers. Earlier this week, Greece’s government submitted an amendment to effectively block European prosecutors from investigating parliamentarians — a highly sensitive subject because of a major probe into political involvement in a massive farm fraud scandal . Members of Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis’ conservative

stories.title: US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García
stories.summary: Judge said Trump administration would not have prosecuted Ábrego had he not challenged his high-profile deportation Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A US judge dismissed a criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García on Friday, finding that the Trump administration would not have prosecuted him had he not challenged his high-profile deportation. Ábrego, who had entered the United States without authorization in the past, became a symbol of the Trump administration’s drive fo

stories.title: NATO chief welcomes Trump decision to send troops to Poland
stories.summary: NATO foreign ministers are meeting in Sweden after Washington apparently reversed its plans to reduce its troop deployments to Poland. Meanwhile, Germany says defense spending will cross 4% of GDP.

stories.title: Trump fan Janša wins new term as Slovenia’s prime minister
stories.summary: Slovenia’s National Assembly on Friday handed nationalist politician Janez Janša his fourth term as prime minister. Earlier this week, Janša announced that his Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) had formed a coalition with several right-wing parties in the Assembly, clearing a path to Slovenia’s top job. Janša was backed by 51 out of 87 deputies who voted in the National Assembly on Friday. The return of Janša, an longtime admirer of U.S. President Donald Trump, gives right-wing populi

stories.title: 250 illegálisan tuningolt autót szűrtek ki Budapesten két év alatt
stories.summary: Egy év alatt felére csökkent a halálos közúti balesetek száma Budapesten. Az illegális gyorsulási versenyek és a rolleres balesetek viszont új típusú problémát jelentenek.

stories.title: Az Euronews szerint nyugdíj- és adóreformon megy a vita az EU-pénzeknél, ezekkel Orbánéknak rég végezniük kellett volna
stories.summary: Az Orbán-kormány által vállalt helyreállítási terv alapján tavaly már hatályba kellett volna lépnie egy nyugdíjrendszert módosító jogszabálynak, a különadókat 2023 végére kellett volna kivezetni.

stories.title: A CERN főigazgatója a Telexnek: Az új kormányon múlik, hogy kihasználják-e a CERN adta lehetőségeket
stories.summary: A CERN Budapesten tartott rendkívüli ülést, ahol megegyeztek abban, milyen részecskeütköztetők születnek majd a jövőben. Mark Thomson, a szervezet főigazgatója a Telexnek azt mondta: ha továbbra is ambiciózus részecskefizikai programot viszünk, Európa a tudomány és a technológia élvonalában maradhat.

stories.title: A NATO-főtitkárral tárgyalt Orbán Anita külügyminiszter
stories.summary: A megbeszélés egyik fő témája az együttműködés erősítése volt.

stories.title: A kormány visszavonja Magyarország kilépési szándékát a Nemzetközi Büntetőbíróságból
stories.summary: A Fidesz tavaly szavazta meg, hogy Magyarország kilépjen az ICC-ből, Magyar Péter viszont már előre jelezte, hogy nekik szándékuk bennmaradni.

stories.title: Hoki-vb: Négygólos vereség a németektől
stories.summary: A magyar csapat így három vereséggel és egy győzelemmel áll.

stories.title: Kósa Lajos visszalépett, új elnöke lett a Magyar Országos Korcsolyázó Szövetségnek
stories.summary: A Fidesz alelnöke tizenöt éven át vezette a szövetséget, a pénteki közgyűlésen Telegdi Attilát választották az utódjáának.

stories.title: Hoki-vb: Németország más szint, 6–2-re verte Magyarországot
stories.summary: Két harmad után 4–0-ra vezettek az eddig nyeretlen németek.

stories.title: Visszaállították az ukrán élelmiszerek behozatali tilalmát
stories.summary: Nem hozható be Magyarországra ukrán búza, kukorica, hús, tojás, méz, bor.

stories.title: Tizenhat év után már nem a felcsúti focicsapat alapítványa fogja üzemeltetni az Alcsúti Arborétumot
stories.summary: Az üzemeltetés 2010-ben került az Orbán Viktor által alapított és Mészáros Lőrinc által elnökölt alapítvány kezébe.

stories.title: Hétköznap nincs dáridó: rendőrt hívtak a szicíliaiak a mulatozó Mick Jaggerre
stories.summary: Másnapra megbánták, hogy megbírságolták a zenészt.

stories.title: Sajtóértesülések szerint Robbie Keane távozik a Ferencvárostól
stories.summary: Az ír edző bajnoki címet és Magyar Kupát is nyert a Fradival, az Európa-ligában pedig a nyolcaddöntőig jutottak.

stories.title: A választás után sem módosított Magyarország adósságbesorolásán a Moody's
stories.summary: A hitelminősítő szerint az új kormány alatt várhatóan javul a viszonyrendszer az Európai Unióval.

stories.title: A politikailag lehetetlent teljesítették, a gazdasági csodára egy kicsit még várni kell
stories.summary: A körvonalazódó gazdaságpolitika a korábbinál sokkal sikeresebb gazdasági pályát alapozhat meg. Ez elvezethet a magyar euróhoz, de a négy-öt éven belüli csatlakozás valóságos csoda lenne.

stories.title: Latvia scrambles NATO jets in drone alert, the latest of several in Baltic
stories.summary: Latvia's military said NATO planes responded to at least one drone flying in its airspace. It's the latest in a string of such incidents in the Baltic, one of which eventually toppled Latvia's government.

stories.title: Afrikai sertéspestis miatt fertőzött területté vált Isaszeg
stories.summary: A hatóságok fokozott óvatosságra kérik a sertéstartókat. A járvány ugyan emberre nem veszélyes, de a sertésekre rendkívül fertőző, ezért szigorú járványvédelmi szabályokat vezettek be.

stories.title: Valakik rájuk küldték a TEK-et, pedig nem jelentettek nemzetbiztonsági kockázatot az ukrán pénzszállítók
stories.summary: A választások előtt az Orbán-kormány és a médiája hetekig lebegtetett konteókat az állítólagos „aranykonvojról”, amiből aztán semmi sem igazolódott, végül a pénzt is visszaadták. A hét ukrán férfit az Alkotmányvédelmi Hivatal véleménye alapján utasították ki Magyarországról, de ezt csak azok nem ismerhetik meg, akiket igazán érint.

stories.title: Hetedik éve tart az úzvölgyi konfliktus: ismét felvonult a katonatemetőben a román szélsőjobb
stories.summary: Az úzvölgyi temető évek óta a román–magyar emlékezetpolitikai konfliktus egyik legismertebb erdélyi helyszíne.

stories.title: BBC World Service to launch news service in Hungarian soon
stories.summary: The previous Hungarian-language service of the BBC was discontinued at the end of 2005.

stories.title: A Fidesz már diktatúrában érzi magát, de épp miattuk nincs kontroll az Alaptörvény-módosítások felett
stories.summary: Stánicz Péter alkotmányjogász szerint a Tisza Párt Alaptörvény-módosítási javaslatában szó sincs visszamenőleges hatályú jogalkotásról, ám ez pont egy olyan kérdés, amit az Alkotmánybíróságnak kellene vizsgálnia. Az ő szerepüket évekkel ezelőtt a Fidesz iktatta ki, miközben most ellenzékben lex Orbánnak nevezik a javaslatot.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint az orosz–ukrán háború végével visszatérhet az EU az orosz gáz vásárlásához
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint a versenyképességhez alacsony energiaárakra van szükség, attól pedig, hogy megnyerték a választást, a földrajz még nem változott meg.

stories.title: Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno
stories.summary: The first gathering of Sudeten Germans in Czechia since World War II is set to go ahead this weekend despite a political backlash that has exposed tensions over one of Central Europe's most painful historical legacies.

stories.title: NATO planes down suspected Ukrainian drone over Estonia
stories.summary: A NATO military jet has shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone in Estonian airspace. As Ukraine targets Russia's Baltic oil facilities more often and more intensely, more such drones are straying into allies' territory.

stories.title: Sweden orders 4 frigates from French company Naval Group
stories.summary: Sweden plans to acquire four new frigates starting in 2030 in a deal worth billions. They will be the largest surface warships in Sweden's navy. The newest NATO member is rushing to build up its military capabilities.

stories.title: Hungary's new Prime Minister Magyar seeks to fix Poland ties
stories.summary: Peter Magyar hopes to mend relations with Poland during his first state visit to Warsaw. What lessons could he learn from Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk?

stories.title: Exclusive: Read Trump’s unsigned AI executive order
stories.summary: POLITICO has obtained a draft of the executive order on artificial intelligence that President Donald Trump had been expected to sign Thursday afternoon , only to abruptly postpone the event . The document — available here and below — was meant to address concerns that advanced AI products from companies such as Anthropic could unleash devastating cyberattacks and wreak other havoc if they fell into the wrong hands. It called for creating a voluntary oversight system in which developers of advan

stories.title: Marco Rubio: Trump ‘disappointment’ with Nato will be discussed at summit
stories.summary: Secretary of state suggests July meeting of alliance will be fraught as US demands help in strait of Hormuz Donald Trump is disappointed that Nato allies refused to become more actively involved in attacking Iran, the US secretary of state has said, setting up what could become a fraught summit of the alliance in July. Marco Rubio, meeting with foreign ministers of the military alliance, emphasised that he expected the rift would be discussed at the July meeting in Ankara, making the summit “one

stories.title: US concerned Russian campaign against Baltics could ‘spark into something bigger’, says Rubio – as it happened
stories.summary: The US secretary of state said the US is monitoring Russian allegations against the Baltics Rubio begins with thanks to Sweden for hosting the ministerial meeting. But that’s where the niceties end as says the upcoming Ankara summit will be “one of the more important leaders’ summit in the history of Nato,” as the leaders will have to respond to Trump’s “disappointment” with the alliance’s “response to our operations in the Middle East.” “That will have to be addressed, that won’t be solved or a

stories.title: The turbulent 15 months of Trump’s unlikely US intelligence director
stories.summary: Over 15 months, the ex-Democrat without an intelligence background took actions seemingly meant to flatter Trump Tulsi Gabbard’s tumultuous 15-month tenure as the nation’s top intelligence official ended Friday, when she submitted her resignation as director of national intelligence. Gabbard was an unconventional choice for the role: the former Democrat lacked an intelligence background and her foreign policy views at times diverged with Donald Trump, particularly on questions of overseas milita

stories.title: Sunrise Movement takes credit for disrupting Trump’s New York state rally – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog has now closed. US intelligence director Tulsi Gabbard leaving post after rocky tenure Sign up for the Breaking News US email “There’s broad recognition there are going to be eventually less US troops in Europe than historically,” Rubio says Rubio said he didn’t set the timeline for reducing the number of US troops in Europe , but “it has been an ongoing process that started from the first day of this administration.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Gaza flotilla activists allege sexual assault and rape in Israeli detention
stories.summary: Israeli prison service denies claims of abuse during detention of 430 people trying to take aid to Palestinians Activists released from Israeli custody after being detained on a flotilla trying to take aid to Gaza were subjected to abuse, organisers have alleged, with several hospitalised with injuries and at least 15 reporting sexual assaults, including rape. Israel’s prison service denied the allegations, and Reuters was not able to verify them independently. Continue reading...

stories.title: Wes Streeting insists he can win Labour leadership race despite ‘underdog’ status
stories.summary: In interview with Guardian, former health secretary sets out plans for government, including social care, tax and refugees Wes Streeting has insisted he can win over the Labour left, as he launches a shadow campaign for the party leadership, saying he has “beaten the odds” throughout his life and can do so again. The former health secretary, who called on Keir Starmer to resign as he quit the cabinet last week, warned Labour MPs that drifting on with Starmer in charge risked a Joe Biden situatio

stories.title: PM says Alberta ‘essential’ to Canada as separatists push for independence
stories.summary: Alberta premier calls for referendum on secession after judge ruled initiative to force binding vote invalid The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney , has said that Alberta is “essential” to the country’s future, hours after the province’s leader moved the oil-rich region closer toward a referendum on independence. Separatists in the western province spent months collecting signatures seeking to trigger a binding October vote on seceding from the nation. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Kind of humiliating’: trans community responds to EHRC’s new code of practice
stories.summary: For some transgender men and women – and the campaigners who support them – the updated guidance confirms their worst fears Stephen Whittle was visiting the Chelsea flower show as a birthday treat with his wife on Thursday afternoon. At around the same time, the updated code of practice from the Equality and Human Rights Commission was published. It confirmed, among myriad updates, that single-sex spaces such as toilets and changing rooms must be used on the basis of biological sex, and that tra

stories.title: State-sponsored apprenticeships and decriminalising cannabis on the bill at Victoria’s Labor conference
stories.summary: Jacinta Allan will announce a plan to offer 2,000 apprenticeships at the revived State Electricity Commission, as part of a pre-election push Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Jacinta Allan will use Labor’s final state conference before the election to announce a plan for a government-owned electricity body to hire electrical apprentices to address significant workforce shortages. It will be the first time since the State Electricity Commission was privatised 30 years

stories.title: Police drop case against artist who depicted high-profile Australians in uniforms with Nazi symbols
stories.summary: Michael Agzarian was charged despite internal legal advice that the images were ‘political satire’, court hears Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Police charged an artist who depicted prominent Australians in military uniforms emblazoned with Nazi symbols despite internal legal advice the images were “political satire”, a court has heard. Police dropped the case against Michael Agzarian before the Downing Centre local court on Friday, almost a year after he had been ch

stories.title: Rubio doubtful of diplomacy with Cuba as Trump renews threat of military action
stories.summary: US secretary of state says president would like a negotiated agreement with Havana but likelihood ‘is not high’ The US president, Donald Trump, and the secretary of state, Marco Rubio, on Thursday again raised the spectre of military intervention in Cuba, a renewed threat that takes on greater weight a day after the administration announced criminal charges against Raúl Castro, the island’s former leader. “Other presidents have looked at this for 50, 60 years, doing something,” Trump told report

stories.title: Venezuelan makeup artist who was deported to El Salvador seeks asylum in Spain: ‘I feel safe here’
stories.summary: Andry José Hernández Romero was deported from the US to El Salvador’s notorious Cecot prison before he was allowed to return to Venezuela One of the Venezuelan men sent from the US to El Salvador’s most notorious prison by Donald Trump has moved to Spain to request asylum after concluding that he did not feel safe back home and did not trust US authorities sufficiently to return to fight his legal case. Andry José Hernández Romero left Venezuela for Spain in early February and is due for his fir

stories.title: Australian government plans for ‘worst-case scenario’ retail fuel rationing, documents reveal
stories.summary: Exclusive: With warnings world oil supplies are expected to hit ‘red zone’ by August, the Albanese government has considered its powers to enforce daily purchase limits for motorists Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The federal government had considered plans for retail fuel rationing early in the fuel crisis, documents reveal, as the International Energy Agency warned on Friday that oil markets would enter the “red zone” by August. Contained in documents obtained by

stories.title: US or Iran: Who will win the Hormuz endurance game?
stories.summary: Tehran is betting that its sanctions‑hardened economy can outlast the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Washington, meanwhile, is contending with elevated oil prices, renewed inflation pressure and rising global recession risks.

stories.title: EU, Mexico sign expanded trade deal
stories.summary: The updated accord between the European Union and Mexico removes most remaining barriers to trade and investment. President Sheinbaum said Mexico's respective trade deals with the EU and the US were "not contradictory."

stories.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's director of national intelligence
stories.summary: Tulsi Gabbard was appointed as director of national intelligence at the start of Trump's second term as US president.

stories.title: Russia's Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of hitting student dormitory
stories.summary: Ukraine said it hit Russia's elite Rubicon drone military unit in a Moscow-occupied part of eastern Ukraine.

stories.title: Judge dismisses criminal case against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
stories.summary: A federal judge says the Maryland man who was wrongfully deported last year is being prosecuted for political reasons.

stories.title: Rubio doubtful diplomacy would work with Cuban government as Trump raises threat of military action
stories.summary: Rubio says the White House is very focused on toppling the socialist government of Cuba to address what he says are national security risks. He also stressed that Havana will not be able to outwait the Trump administration, urging them to strike a deal before it is too late.

stories.title: Turkey welcomes 422 Gaza flotilla activists after Israel detention
stories.summary: 422 Gaza flotilla activists landed in Istanbul on Thursday after being deported by Israel. Several alleged physical abuse and torture following the interception of their aid convoy.

stories.title: Russian strike on Sumy injures six as mass drone attack hits Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukraine said six people, including a child, were injured after a Russian strike hit Sumy on Thursday. Firefighters spent hours tackling flames in residential and industrial areas damaged in the attack.

stories.title: Merz, Meloni and allies castigate Israel over West Bank settler violence
stories.summary: The leaders of France, Germany, Italy and the U.K. on Friday urged Israel to halt the expansion of settlements in the West Bank and curb rising settler violence. “Over the past few months, the situation in the West Bank has deteriorated significantly. Settler violence is at unprecedented levels,” German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said in a blunt joint statement . “The policies and prac

stories.title: Tony Blair’s consultancy doubles down on AI and restructures in Europe
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The consulting firm led by former U.K. Prime Minister Tony Blair is increasing its focus on artificial intelligence and overhauling its European presence. The firm is set to introduce a new center for “leading in the age of AI” this fall, as it seeks to become the leading partner for governments navigating the global AI race. The renewed focus on AI comes with a total revamp of its organization. The Tony Blair Institute, of which Blair is executive chairman, is “evol

stories.title: Judith Chalmers, presenter of TV series Wish You Were Here, dies aged 90
stories.summary: Family says host of more than 500 episodes of travel show leaves behind ‘giant suitcase of the happiest memories’ The TV presenter Judith Chalmers, who spent almost three decades persuading British people to go on holiday as the host of Wish You Were Here … ?, has died at the age of 90 after living with Alzheimer’s in her final years. Her family said she died peacefully at home on Thursday, surrounded by “the family she loved so much”, after becoming ill in recent weeks. They added that she woul

stories.title: The Dreamed Adventure review – beautiful but opaque Bulgarian tale of digging up the past
stories.summary: Cannes film festival: Valeska Grisebach’s complex drama tracks an archaeologist whose mountain dig is interrupted by an old friend with rather dirtier hands The digging up of the past – and the hiding of secrets in the present – are the themes of Valeska Grisebach’s complex, subtle, opaque new drama which seems to withhold some of its narrative meaning from the audience, moment-by-moment. It is set, like her previous film Western , in Bulgaria’s remote and beautiful mountainous country, where me

stories.title: Suspected Ebola cases triple in a week as WHO warns of rapid spread in DRC
stories.summary: Situation described as ‘deeply worrisome’ by officials as aid cuts and community distrust impede responders The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo poses a “very high” risk to the country, the World Health Organization said on Friday, revising its threat assessment upwards. The outbreak is spreading rapidly, WHO leaders said, with almost 750 suspected cases and 177 suspected deaths, up from 246 cases and 65 deaths when it was first reported a week earlier. Continue reading...

stories.title: Soft power sell-off: anger as British Council announces sale of historic Madrid building
stories.summary: Backlash grows among European staff against radical cuts to pay off Covid-era debt, with some accusing council of ‘colonial attitude’ The historic Palacete building at 31 Paseo del General Martínez Campos in Madrid’s upmarket Chamberí district has been home to the British Council in Spain for about 70 years. About 5,000 students each year pass through its 35 classrooms, learning English, attending exams, and forging cultural ties with the UK. Over the years that is hundreds of thousands of Madri

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: The Ebola outbreak, Israeli strikes in Gaza, Putin in Beijing and Arsenal win the Premier League – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: The Birthday Party review – grimly compulsive unhappy occasion in deepest France
stories.summary: Cannes film festival: This could be better paced but the crisis which descends on an up-against-it dairy farm is delivered by some very memorable goons There’s nothing like a home-invasion suspense thriller to provide a change of pace in the Cannes competition, and Léa Mysius’s film – adapted from the French bestseller Histoires de la Nuit by Laurent Mauvignier – isn’t at all bad, although it runs out of narrative steam in the third act and one particular shock-twist appears to unshock and untwi

stories.title: UK pitched single market for goods with EU in pursuit of deeper trade ties
stories.summary: Exclusive: Top British official presented idea in Brussels but sources say it was rebuffed The UK government pitched the creation of a single market for goods with the EU as the cornerstone of an ambitious attempt to reintegrate British trade back into Europe, the Guardian can reveal. During recent visits to Brussels, the Cabinet Office’s top official on EU relations, Michael Ellam, presented the idea to deepen the UK’s economic relationship with the bloc. Continue reading...

stories.title: SpaceX launches its biggest rocket yet in test flight from Texas
stories.summary: The launch is the 12th test flight of the mega-rocket that CEO Elon Musk is building to get people to Mars one day SpaceX launched its biggest, most powerful Starship yet on a test flight Friday, an upgraded version that Nasa is counting on to land astronauts on the moon. The redesigned mega-rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he’s taking the company public . It blasted off from the southern tip of Texas, carrying 20 mock Starlink satellites for release halfway ar

stories.title: California: 40,000 people ordered to evacuate over chemical leak fears
stories.summary: Authorities in Orange county say tank holding methyl methacrylate ‘actively in crisis’ and urge residents to leave Authorities in Orange county, California have ordered the evacuation of 40,000 people over concerns about a chemical leak that threatened to spill or explode. The problem arose on Thursday at a facility owned by GKN Aerospace in the town of Garden Grove, where a storage tank holding methyl methacrylate began off-gassing and threatened to fail. The chemical, which is highly flammable

stories.title: Qatar sends mediators to Tehran in sign talks to reopen strait of Hormuz are reaching climax
stories.summary: Iran has threatened to impose tolls on shipping, while US demands that Iran hand over its highly enriched uranium may be deferred. Is there an end in sight? Qatar has rushed a team of mediators to Tehran in a sign that talks to open the strait of Hormuz, in return for the lifting of US sanctions and asset freezes, are reaching a climax. The aim would be to sign a memorandum of understanding on the strait that would lead to 30 days of talks on Iran’s nuclear programme – so deferring discussion of

stories.title: Three die in ‘horrific’ south Sydney vehicle crash after police welfare check
stories.summary: A 42-year-old fled from police before being involved in a two-vehicle crash in Sans Souci, police say Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Three people have died in a crash in Sydney after a man fled police who had been attempting to carry out a welfare check. A 42-year-old man fled in a white Audi after officers were called to a welfare check at a property in Gymea Bay, southern Sydney, at 12.25am on Saturday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump Mobile investigating potential exposure of would-be customers’ personal information
stories.summary: Phone company launched by Donald Trump’s family says names and contact details appear to be affected, but not credit card or banking information A phone company launched by Donald Trump’s family business is investigating a potential security flaw on its website that appears to have exposed the personal details of an estimated 27,000 people who sought to buy a gold-coloured smartphone. Trump Mobile said in a statement that it was investigating the issue – “with the assistance of independent cyber

stories.title: US arms sales to Taiwan on ‘pause’ due to Iran war, says acting navy chief
stories.summary: Hung Cao’s comments are latest blow to Taipei after Donald Trump recently cast doubt over US’s enduring support US arms sales to Taiwan have been “paused” to ensure the US military has enough munitions for its Iran operations, according to Washington’s acting navy secretary, in the latest blow to Taipei after a series of comments by Donald Trump. When asked at a congressional hearing on Thursday about a $14bn (£10.4bn) weapons package awaiting Trump’s signoff for months, Hung Cao said: “Right no

stories.title: Electoral reform and reversing Brexit: they’re more connected than you might think | Tom Baldwin
stories.summary: Labour’s emerging leadership contest is reopening the EU debate. But if we want to rejoin, Britain needs a more European voting system first Nowhere is an anniversary more relished than in newspapers. As we approach the 10-year mark since Britain voted for Brexit, countless column inches would no doubt have been reserved for this purpose anyway. Yet the prospect of a Labour leadership contest, at a time when polls are showing four-fifths of the party’s voters at the last election and an even hig

stories.title: Flotilla video: Ben-Gvir’s template of televised abuse was honed on Palestinians
stories.summary: Targeting of foreign activists drew global outrage from governments that have not acted on violence against Palestinian detainees Israel’s far-right national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, has made abuse of detained Palestinians something of a macabre calling card, celebrating cruelty publicly and often on video. During his time in office, violence including rape , extreme hunger and humiliation have been normalised in Israeli jails. Rights groups say detention centres have become “ torture

stories.title: Iran war: 'Slight progress' in peace talks, Rubio says
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Rubio said there was "a little bit of movement" in negotiations to end the Iran war. Meanwhile, four European nations called on Israel to stop the expansion of West Bank settlements.

stories.title: What is Pentecost?
stories.summary: People new to Germany are often surprised by the number of public holidays in the spring. Pentecost is one of them, but its significance is not well known.

stories.title: US: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's intelligence chief
stories.summary: Tulsi Gabbard has resigned as head of US intelligence, citing her husband's recent cancer diagnosis. An opponent of foreign US interventions, Gabbard has sought to distance herself from the US-Israeli war on Iran.

stories.title: New alliances emerge in Middle East — which one will 'win'?
stories.summary: The UAE and Israel are getting closer, while Saudi Arabia is enhancing cooperation with Egypt, Turkey and Pakistan. The new alliances represent a rift in the Gulf, as approaches to the Iran war and the region diverge.

stories.title: Kevin Warsh: Trump ally at the Federal Reserve?
stories.summary: Never before has a chair of the US Federal Reserve been so controversial. Who is Kevin Warsh? Can he preserve the Fed's independence, or will he become Donald Trump's puppet?

stories.title: SpaceX launches biggest Starship rocket yet in test flight
stories.summary: SpaceX's 12th Starship test flight took place just two days after Elon Musk announced that he was taking the company public.

stories.title: India swats cockroach movement over 'national security'
stories.summary: After the Cockroach Janta Party's X handle was blocked in India, reports say its Instagram handle is also in danger of suspension. Meanwhile, the Quad defense grouping is set to convene in India. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: What happened to jailed Belarus dissident Raman Pratasevich?
stories.summary: In 2021, Belarus diverted a Ryanair plane to seize opposition journalist Raman Pratasevich. What has happened to him since, and why is he now seemingly supporting the Belarusian regime?

stories.title: Bigger than ever: WUF13 ends with global call for action
stories.summary: As the World Urban Forum ended in Baku on Friday, the debate on the future of cities evolved beyond architecture, infrastructure and skylines to the urgent global question of how can cities withstand conflict, climate change, rapid urbanisation and inequality without leaving communities behind.

stories.title: Waymo pauses robotaxis in five US cities after cars drive into flooded roads
stories.summary: A Waymo spokesperson said it had expanded the temporary pause "out of an abundance of caution".

stories.title: Ebola risk raised to 'very high' in DR Congo
stories.summary: The head of the UN health agency says the risk in the wider region is "high", but it remains "low" at the global level.

stories.title: Bodies of Italian divers did not have optimal equipment, says rescuer
stories.summary: Images from a cave where the four Italians were found reveal the difficulty that rescuers had in recovering their bodies.

stories.title: Trump is putting pressure on Cuba - why and to what end?
stories.summary: The US has warned that a peaceful agreement with Cuba is unlikely, Havana insists this is a "fraudulent case" designed to justify military intervention.

stories.title: UAE-backed programme broadens assessment of public institutions
stories.summary: Joint Uzbek and Emirati evaluation teams reviewed ministries and agencies using criteria including transparency, digitalisation and service delivery. Officials say future assessments will place greater emphasis on measurable results.

stories.title: Tulsi Gabbard to resign as US director of national intelligence
stories.summary: Gabbard who has been largely out of public view during recent US operations says she is leaving due to her husband's illness.

stories.title: Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse by Israeli forces while detained
stories.summary: The detainees say they were slapped, hit, humiliated and even subjected to sexual violence, but Israel's prison service says the allegations are false.

stories.title: Carney says Alberta is 'essential' to Canada as province plans vote on separation
stories.summary: Alberta's decision to hold a referendum on separation has been widely criticised, including by separatists who say the question is not definitive enough.

stories.title: 'Speed, money and compassion' - lessons from an Ebola survivor and other experts
stories.summary: Those caught up in West Africa's Ebola outbreak a decade ago on how best to tackle the current epidemic.

stories.title: SpaceX launches massive Starship V3 rocket on test flight
stories.summary: The first attempted launch on Thursday was postponed due to a malfunctioning hydraulic pin in the launch tower.

stories.title: More than 40,000 Californians evacuated due to chemical tank leak
stories.summary: Officials have warned that the tank contains a "highly volatile" substance that could lead to an explosion.

stories.title: Erdoğan signs decree closing Istanbul's Bilgi University amid criminal probe into owner
stories.summary: As part of an investigation into Can Holding, the operating licence of Istanbul Bilgi University was revoked after its founding foundation was placed under trusteeship. The decision has raised concerns about the future of its students and academic staff.

stories.title: Greece protesters marched in Athens outside Israeli embassy
stories.summary: Around 1,000 pro-Palestinian demonstrators marched through Athens on Thursday after Israël intercepted a flotilla carrying Gaza activists, gathering outside the Israeli embassy as international criticism over the arrests mounted.

stories.title: Ukraine strike on Starobilsk dormitory kills four in occupied Luhansk
stories.summary: Ukrainian drone strike on a college dorm in Russian‑occupied Starobilsk killed four and wounded at least 35, while Russian drones also hit residential areas in Kherson.

stories.title: Starmer says he will campaign for Burnham in Makerfield byelection – as it happened
stories.summary: Prime minister tells reporters he would campaign personally in the contest The Home Office has also published asylum figures this morning. These show that the number of asylum seekers being housed temporarily in hotels stood at 20,885 at the end of March 2026, down 35% year-on-year from 32,326. The Press Association says: It is the lowest figure since data was first reported in 2022, Home Office figures show. The total had climbed as high as 56,018 at the end of September 2023. Brits are leaving

stories.title: Cubans outraged at US charges against Raúl Castro as fears of military strikes grow
stories.summary: It’s a nerve-racking time for Havana neighbours of top Cuban officials as fears of US attack mount A new question in being asked in Havana as people digest the news that the US has brought criminal charges against Cuba’s 94-year-old former president, Raúl Castro: who’s your neighbour? If you happen to live near a senior figure in Cuba’s government or armed forces, others suck their teeth in an expression of concerned sympathy. For the first time, US military strikes on the island are being consi

stories.title: Turkey: DW's Alican Uludag released, but trial continues
stories.summary: A Turkish court has allowed DW reporter Alican Uludag to leave detention as his contentious trial opens. He is accused of having "insulted" President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, which in Turkey can carry a jail sentence.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery in long-range drone attack, Kyiv says
stories.summary: Ukraine has been ramping up its retaliatory long-range strikes on Russia in recent weeks, particularly targeting oil infrastructure.

stories.title: Israel says all Gaza flotilla activists have been deported amid global outrage over their treatment
stories.summary: Multiple European countries are calling for EU sanctions against Israeli national security minister Itamar Ben Gvir.

stories.title: Turkish court annuls leadership of main opposition CHP party, sparking turmoil
stories.summary: The case concerns allegations of vote buying at the CHP congress in November 2023, with prosecutors alleging that Özgür Özel secured his election as party leader with promises of jobs and other kickbacks.

stories.title: Taliban ‘legitimising child marriage’ with new law, activists warn
stories.summary: Up to 70% of girls may be in early or forced marriages but law now makes divorce impossible if husbands disagree Child marriage appears to have been legally recognised for the first time by the Taliban in Afghanistan, as activists say “shameful” new laws make it almost impossible for girls and young women to seek divorce against their husbands’ will. There are no official statistics on forced and underage marriages in Afghanistan, but activists say it has risen at an alarming rate in recent year

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China
stories.summary: Twelve dead as southern and central areas also suffer travel disruption, electricity outages and evacuations Heavy, prolonged rainfall in southern and central China has resulted in weather warnings for flash flooding, landslides and waterlogging. The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday. It was formed from the convergence of multiple bands of rain originating from the Bay of Bengal, South China Sea and Pacific Ocean. Given

stories.title: Tony Abbott to become new Liberal party president in former PM’s return to political frontlines
stories.summary: Unopposed nomination comes seven years after Abbott lost his Sydney seat of Warringah at the 2019 election Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tony Abbott will become the new federal Liberal party president in a return to the political frontlines for the conservative former prime minister. Abbott was the only nominee for the presidency after the other contender – former foreign minister Alexander Downer – chose to instead run for one of the vice-president’s positions. Co

stories.title: UN’s climate crisis vote shows political momentum is growing, say experts
stories.summary: Resolution backed by 141 states hailed as ‘new chapter’ that could improve climate diplomacy and litigation efforts When the UN general assembly voted overwhelmingly in favour of a landmark climate crisis ruling on Wednesday, the Pacific island of Vanuatu’s prime minister hailed the result as the start of “a new chapter” in climate action. “The task before all of us now is to translate legal clarity into meaningful action, stronger cooperation, and greater protection for present and future gener

stories.title: Meta and Snapchat blocking Saudi dissidents’ accounts
stories.summary: US social media firms acting on orders from Middle East kingdom accused of being ‘instruments of repression’ Major US social media companies including Meta’s Facebook and Instagram platforms have blocked the accounts of Saudi Arabian dissidents so they are no longer visible inside the kingdom, following orders by Saudi authorities. Those affected include Abdullah Alaoudh, a US-based activist and vocal critic of Saudi human rights violations, and Omar Abdulaziz, a Canada and UK-based activist who

stories.title: DW exclusive: Germany's far-right AfD vows to 'make history'
stories.summary: The AfD looks set to take power in a German state for the first time. In a DW exclusive, the front-runner in Saxony-Anhalt discusses plans to detain immigrants and revamp the school system, and relations with Russia.

stories.title: How the Cannes 2026 films reflect a world in conflict
stories.summary: Various works explore the impact of authoritarianism through a historical lens, while one thriller on corruption is set in Putin's Russia.

stories.title: Cyprus election expected to shake up national politics
stories.summary: Cyprus goes to the polls to elect a new parliament on May 24. The vote is expected to reshape the country's political landscape, weaken traditional parties and accelerate the rise of new anti-establishment movements.

stories.title: Can the Quad stay relevant to boost Indo-Pacific security?
stories.summary: Foreign ministers from the US, India, Japan and Australia — collectively known as the Quad — need to put past differences behind them if the alliance is to remain an effective means of countering China.

stories.title: Ebola: Experts warn of scale and spread of deadly virus
stories.summary: Health experts have warned the world is seeing just the "top of the iceberg" as the deadly Ebola outbreak spreads. Meanwhile, India and the African Union have postponed a scheduled summit due to the health emergency.

stories.title: The dying dream of the dead to be at peace with life
stories.summary: Many people have vivid dreams shortly before death. Research suggests the dreams are not a sign of confusion — but may help both the dying and their loved ones make sense of loss.

stories.title: US bourbon bets big on India's growing market
stories.summary: Bourbon, the uniquely US take on whiskey, is facing its most serious inventory crisis in decades. The industry is increasingly looking to India to boost sales.

stories.title: South Africa xenophobia response under fire
stories.summary: South Africa condemns xenophobic violence when it erupts, but critics say prevention is weak, arrests are limited and political rhetoric sometimes fuels tensions instead of easing them.

stories.title: Germany news: Parliament approves reforms giving pharmacies more autonomy on vaccinations, prescriptions, tests
stories.summary: Lawmakers passed a bill allowing pharmacies to issue certain prescription medications independently as well as to conduct more rapid tests. And bees besiege a Beetle in Düsseldorf's rush hour.

stories.title: Republican lawmakers call off vote on Trump's War Powers Resolution that was on the verge of passing
stories.summary: Democrats slammed Republicans as “cowards” for delaying the voting procedure into next month, while Republicans argue the move was to allow lawmakers unable to attend the vote a chance to cast their say.

stories.title: ‘We are not for sale’: Greenlanders protest growing US presence in Nuuk
stories.summary: Hundreds in Nuuk protest a renewed US presence, accusing Washington of treating Greenland as a strategic asset and demanding respect for self-rule.

stories.title: British climber scales Everest for record 20th time, officials say
stories.summary: Two Indian climbers are believed to have died while descending the mountain this week, after climbing the peak on Thursday.

stories.title: Angry crowd sets fire to Ebola treatment center in Congo
stories.summary: Residents in eastern Congo set fire to an Ebola treatment center after authorities stopped them from retrieving the body of a suspected victim for burial, as anger and fear grow over the worsening outbreak. Aid workers fled the scene as flames engulfed the facility.

stories.title: Furious crowd torches Ebola hospital tents in DR Congo
stories.summary: Rwampara hospital lies near Bunia in the DRC's Ituri province, the epicentre of the Ebola outbreak.

stories.title: India faces extreme heat as dead fish wash up in New Delhi
stories.summary: India’s severe heatwave is affecting both people and wildlife in New Delhi. Hundreds of dead fish were removed from Sanjay Lake on Friday as temperatures remained far above seasonal averages.

stories.title: Timelapse footage shows 'giant cave' inflating on Paris bridge
stories.summary: The monumental structure was created by Parisan artist JR - often dubbed the French Banksy

stories.title: Why thousands of stock trades tied to Trump are raising eyebrows
stories.summary: The BBC's Michelle Fleury looks at trades disclosed by the president.

stories.title: How Cubans feel about the US indictment of Raúl Castro
stories.summary: People in Havana reacted after US prosecutors indicted former Cuban leader Raúl Castro over the 1996 shootdown of planes operated by a Miami-based exile group, with several residents calling for dialogue instead of confrontation.

stories.title: Iran asserts jurisdiction over UAE and Oman waters in new Strait of Hormuz map
stories.summary: Five Gulf states have formally warned shipping companies through the IMO not to comply, as analysts say Iran is deliberately expanding its claimed zone beyond even its own initial boundaries in a step-by-step assertion of territorial control.

stories.title: An Indian bride dies. Rival claims of murder and suicide set off media frenzy
stories.summary: Twisha Sharma, a model and actor, had been married for just five months when she was found dead.

stories.title: Taiwan says it has received no notification from US of any pause to planned €12bn arms sale
stories.summary: Like other countries that maintain formal diplomatic ties with Beijing, the US doesn't recognise Taiwan as a country, but Washington remains the island’s strongest backer and arms supplier.

stories.title: Lebanon opens seafront tent camp in Beirut as Israeli strikes displace 1 million
stories.summary: Lebanon has opened a large tent camp on the seafront in Beirut to shelter civilians displaced by Israeli bombardment and fighting linked to Hezbollah, as more than one million people have now been forced from their homes.

stories.title: USA: MomoCon in Atlanta unites 65,000 fans for a huge geek culture fest
stories.summary: MomoCon opened this week in Atlanta, with organisers expecting around 65,000 gamers, anime fans and cosplayers to attend one of the country’s biggest celebrations of geek culture, providing a major boost to local businesses.

stories.title: Sabalenka cuts media short as French Open players protest
stories.summary: Leading players, including Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff and Jannik Sinner, limit their French Open media duties to 15 minutes in a row over prize money.

stories.title: Hivatalos: október 19-én népszavazást tartanak Albertában a Kanadától való elszakadásról
stories.summary: A közvélemény-kutatások szerint az albertaiak többsége a szakadás ellen szavazna, a referendum mégis próbára teheti Kanada egységét.

stories.title: A MOME szenátusa nyilatkozatban szólítja fel elszámolásra az egyetem kuratóriumát
stories.summary: A testület szerint a MOME kuratóriuma önállóan már nem hozhat az egyetem működésének biztosításán túlmutató döntéseket.

stories.title: Csak illúzió, hogy az ember valóban képes irányítani a saját sorsát
stories.summary: A közelmúltban elhunyt Dag Solstad trilógiája az elmúlt évek egyik legkülönösebb és legmélyebb szépirodalmi teljesítménye. Kritika.

stories.title: Ki az a Mága Zoltán? – kérdezte tőlünk a zenész, akinek a neve szintén szerepel a hegedűművész pályázatában
stories.summary: Hat zenésszel beszéltünk, akinek a nevét feltüntették Mágáék NKA-nak beadott, 500 milliós pályázatában, de egyikükkel sem egyeztettek erről előzetesen. Desh, Király Linda és Nótár Mary is tőlünk tudta meg, hogy a tervezett előadók listáján volt.

stories.title: Negyedszer is Janez Janša lesz Szlovénia miniszterelnöke
stories.summary: Janša csak a NATO-ellenes és oroszbarát Resnica támogatásával tudott többséget szerezni.

stories.title: Vizsgálatot rendelt el a 45 milliárdos tankönyvszerződés ügyében Lannert Judit
stories.summary: A miniszter azt ígérte, megteszik a szükséges lépéseket, ha a vizsgálat jogi, pénzügyi vagy közbeszerzési problémát tár fel.

stories.title: Távozik az Országos Vérellátó Szolgálat főigazgatója, Bayerné Matusovits Andrea
stories.summary: Főigazgatóként korábban versenyeztetés nélkül, árun alul adott el vérplazmát magáncégeknek. Utódjának az egyik legfontosabb feladata Hegedűs Zsolt szerint az intézmény teljes átvilágítása lesz.

stories.title: Férje betegsége miatt lemond az amerikai nemzeti hírszerzés igazgatója, Tulsi Gabbard
stories.summary: Donald Trump szerint Gabbard hihetetlen munkát végzett, és hiányozni fog nekik. Ideiglenesen Gabbard helyettese, Aaron Lukas veszi át az igazgatói pozíciót.

stories.title: Novák egyenes vonallal húzta át K. Endre elutasító határozatát – ezek a kegyelmi ügyben kiadott dokumentumok legérdekesebb részei
stories.summary: A Sándor-palota jogi csapata részletesen érvelt Nováknak, miért nem szabadna kegyelmet adni K. Endrének, hogy aztán a normális ügymenettől teljesen eltérve egy támogató és egy elutasító határozatot is a kezébe nyomjanak. A volt köztársasági elnök az egyiket aláírta, a másikat kihúzta.

stories.title: A Tisza devizahiteles törvényjavaslata egyelőre épp annyira kedvez a bankoknak, mint az adósoknak
stories.summary: A Tisza szerdán benyújtott törvényjavaslata felfüggesztené a devizahiteles pereket és végrehajtásokat, amíg nem születik rendezés az ügyben, de annak szövegezése több problémát is tartalmaz devizahitelekkel foglalkozó ügyvédek szerint.

stories.title: Két hónappal a választás előtt Gulyás Gergely 300 milliós támogatást adott a Mesterséges Intelligencia Kutatóközpontnak
stories.summary: Ez a szervezet nevezte hamisnak a Tisza Párt október 23-i rendezvényéről készült légi felvételt.

stories.title: Rogán megszűnő minisztériuma ezúttal elektronikusan adott ki közadatokat
stories.summary: Az aHang nemzeti petícióról szóló igénylésére válaszul elküldték a Balásy Gyula cégeivel kötött keretszerződés linkjét is.

stories.title: A Mol további két hetet kapott a NIS ügyében zajló tárgyalások lezárására
stories.summary: A szerb energiaügyi miniszter szerint a helyzet összetett.

stories.title: Piros zászló és Russell visszavágása a Kanadai Nagydíj sprintidőmérőjén
stories.summary: A Mercedesnek a jelek szerint bejöttek a fejlesztések, Hamilton remeklésébe egy utolsó körös hiba rondított bele.

stories.title: Pedro Sánchez is loved everywhere – but not so much in Spain. Here’s why | María Ramírez
stories.summary: The PM’s socialist party was crushed in its former Andalusian stronghold. With a general election in 2027, time to turn things around is running out Lately, I often meet people outside Spain who praise the prime minister, Pedro Sánchez. In Britain, Italy or the US, friends, acquaintances or random people who learn I am Spanish offer admiring words about his positions on Gaza and Iran. It’s understandable. Sánchez spoke out against Benjamin Netanyahu and Donald Trump earlier and more forcefully t

stories.title: Parody Cockroach Janta political party’s rise reflects youth anger in India
stories.summary: Satirical project is viral sensation and outlet for protest on social media as it taps into young people’s frustration It began as a satirical online project after India’s chief justice compared unemployed young people to cockroaches. Now millions of young Indians are flocking to it as an outlet for their frustration. A parody political party with the insect as its symbol has exploded across India’s social media by turning absurdist humour into protest. Memes and short videos mocking corruption,

stories.title: Netanyahu speaks out after video shows minister taunting activists
stories.summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla set sail from Turkey last week in an effort to breach Israel's blockade of Gaza.

stories.title: Israeli security minister Ben Gvir releases footage of detained flotilla activists
stories.summary: Video released by Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir showed detained Gaza flotilla activists handcuffed on the ground at Ashdod port.

stories.title: Trump envoy says US should restore its 'footprint' in Greenland
stories.summary: The US is reported to have had 17 military facilities and more than 10,000 troops on Greenland at the height of the Cold War.

stories.title: Tehran politicians are holding back military from striking US fleet, Iranian MP tells Euronews
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview, a senior member of Iran's parliamentary National Security Commission tells Euronews that political authorities have actively restrained the military from attacking US naval assets — but warns Tehran will sink a US warship if that check is gone.

stories.title: Israel destroyed a four storey house in overnight airstrike in central Gaza camp
stories.summary: Israel destroyed a four storey house in Gaza’s Maghazi refugee camp after residents received a warning call ordering evacuation before the strike.

stories.title: Cities must plan for migration as permanent reality, experts say at World Urban Forum
stories.summary: Governments must treat migration as a permanent feature of urban planning, not an emergency, the IOM deputy director general told Euronews at the World Urban Forum in Baku.

stories.title: 'No means no': Greenlanders protest against Trump outside new US consulate
stories.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.

stories.title: US is ‘simply choosing not to stop’ Ebola outbreak after massive public health cuts, experts say
stories.summary: Hundreds of cases reported in the DRC after USAID has been dismantled and key scientific research canceled A previously undetected outbreak of Ebola is coursing through parts of central Africa, and the US appears to be doing little to help stop it, after massive cuts to global and domestic public health efforts. There is no cure and no vaccine for the rare Bundibugyo variant of Ebola, which has caused two outbreaks in recent decades. Health leaders and scientists are now racing to understand whe

stories.title: Record 274 climbers summit Everest from Nepalese side in single day
stories.summary: Climbers take advantage of clear weather after threat of ice fall on normal route delayed start of spring season A record 274 climbers have reached the summit of Mount Everest from the Nepalese side in a single day after a spring season that started late because of the threat of ice fall on the normal tourist route. The climbers took advantage of the clear weather on Wednesday, said Rishi Ram Bhandari, of the Expedition Operators Association Nepal. Continue reading...

stories.title: Macron says France must address issue of reparations for its role in slave trade
stories.summary: French president’s use of term comes as demand grows for formal discussion on addressing legacies of enslavement Emmanuel Macron has said reparations for France’s role in hundreds of years of enslavement of African people is an issue that should be addressed, but he stopped short of making clear proposals. “How to repair … is a question that must not be refused,” the French president said in a speech on the legacies of slavery at the Élysée Palace. “It’s also a question on which we must not make

stories.title: Ebola: US ban on travellers from DRC, Uganda or South Sudan ‘not the solution’
stories.summary: Africa CDC says restrictions could increase public health risks and highlight ‘deeper structural injustice’ in global health A US travel ban for people coming from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Uganda or South Sudan in response to the Ebola outbreak could make the situation worse, critics have said. The outbreak was declared a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday and continues to spread, with a new case reported in the DRC’s South Kivu province , an area under the c

stories.title: Osztálykiránduló diákokat bántalmazott szexuálisan egy magát biztonsági őrnek tettető férfi, 16 év fegyházat kapott
stories.summary: Az ítélet nem jogerős, a férfi ugyanabban a szállodában szállt meg, ahol a kiránduló diákok.

stories.title: Felmentették a Till Tamás ügyében nyomozó rendőröket, akiket kényszervallatással vádoltak
stories.summary: A bajai fiú meggyilkolásával megvádolt férfi kezdeményezett eljárást, azt állította, fenyegetéssel vették rá, hogy ismerje be a bűncselekményt.

stories.title: Michael Carrick 2028-ig marad a Manchester United élén
stories.summary: Carrick januári kinevezése óta látványos fordulatot hozott a klub játékában, a csapat a bronzéremig kapaszkodott fel a Premier League-ben.

stories.title: Harry Styles úgy tisztelgett Thom Yorke előtt egy díjátadón, hogy elárulta, melyik Radiohead-számra vesztette el a szüzességét
stories.summary: Harry Styles a stadionkoncertjeiről lógott el, hogy önironikus és vicces beszédet mondjon Yorke-ról az egyik legfontosabb dalszerzői díjátadón.

stories.title: Lannert Judit Katz Sándor fizikust kérte fel felsőoktatási államtitkárnak
stories.summary: Katz az elmúlt években az ELTE Fizikai és Csillagászati Intézetének igazgatójaként dolgozott.

stories.title: 37 fokot mértek Spanyolországban, komoly hőség tombol az Ibériai-félszigeten
stories.summary: A forró levegő Közép-Európa felé tart, hamarosan pedig Párizsban és Londonban is 30 fok lehet.

stories.title: Tiszaújvárosi polgármester: A városban látták a füstöt, és azonnal kaptam az értesítést, hogy súlyos baleset történt
stories.summary: Fülöp György szerint ideális volt a kommunikáció a város, a Mol és a katasztrófavédelem között.

stories.title: Ókovács Szilveszter felajánlotta, hogy saját költségén megcsináltatja a házelnöki szék másolatát az Operaház asztalosműhelyével
stories.summary: Az Operaház főigazgatója már elküldte a felajánlást a hivatalos országgyűlési címre. Ókovács szerint a másolattal meg tudnák őrizni az ülésterem történelmi enteriőrjét.

stories.title: Forsthoffer Ágnes a távozó képviselőket is arra kéri, hogy jótékony célra utalják a háromhavi végkielégítésüket
stories.summary: Az állam és a civil szféra helyzetére hivatkozva kéri ezt a házelnök, különösen azoktól, akik a méltatlan állapot kialakulásához hozzájárultak.

stories.title: Tíz éven belül a kutyákba, 15 éven belül a macskákba is csipet kell ültetni az EU-ban
stories.summary: A tagállami kormányok is elfogadták, így húsz napon belül életbe lép a jogszabály a kutyák és macskák azonosíthatóságáról, amely fokozatosan vezeti be a csipbeültetési kötelezettséget.

stories.title: Ismét eladó a Fehérvár FC, június közepéig lehet pályázni a klubra
stories.summary: Júliusra már meg is lehet az új tulajdonos.

stories.title: A hiányzó uniós pénzek és a választási osztogatás miatt állt földbe a költségvetés április végére
stories.summary: Az uniós bevételeknél keletkezett a legnagyobb, közel 1450 milliárd forintos hiány.

stories.title: Berobban a strandszezon: országszerte nyitnak a fürdők a pünkösdi hétvégén
stories.summary: Összeszedtük, hol nyitnak a strandok.

stories.title: Nyomoz a NAV a Nemzeti Kulturális Alap támogatásai ügyében
stories.summary: Költségvetési csalás, valamint hűtlen kezelés bűncselekmény gyanúja miatt folytatnak nyomozást.

stories.title: A Mol-üzemben megsérültek közül két főt már hazaengedtek a miskolci kórházból
stories.summary: A tiszaújvárosi baleset négy súlyos sérültjét Debrecenben ápolják, egyikük lélegeztetésre szorul.

stories.title: Rövid távon nincs hatása a tiszaújvárosi robbanásnak a Mol-csoport termelésére
stories.summary: A tavalyi százhalombattai tűz miatt kevés vegyipari benzint kapott Tiszaújváros, így az Olefin-1 üzem kieső termelését át tudja venni az Olefin-2.

stories.title: Extreme climate scenario fades, but warming continues
stories.summary: The rapid rollout of renewable energy is helping to shift emissions trends, but expected temperature rises remain high as the UN moves to tighten countries' commitments.

stories.title: Will SpaceX's IPO fund life on Mars — and a trillionaire?
stories.summary: SpaceX is launching history's biggest public listing, propelling Elon Musk's wealth into the stratosphere. DW explores whether the firm's out-of-this-world ambitions are truly cosmic or another black hole for investors.

stories.title: Germany reaches out to Gulf states about Iran war
stories.summary: Germany no longer believes it can play a mediating role in the conflict between the US, Israel and Iran. Instead, the German government is working to strengthen its ties with the Gulf states.

stories.title: Will Germany's aviation tax cut lower ticket prices?
stories.summary: The tax cut aims to ease the burden on the aviation industry and make flying slightly cheaper. However, some experts warn that it is unlikely to have a meaningful impact on ticket prices.

stories.title: Ebola patient in Berlin: Why Germany's helping
stories.summary: An Ebola‑infected US doctor is being treated under the highest safety standards in a specialized isolation unit in the German capital, Berlin. The facilities are considered to be among the best equipped worldwide.

stories.title: Lebanon rescuers search rubble after attacks in Tyre province
stories.summary: Rescuers search rubble in southern Lebanon after Israeli strikes kill at least 19, including women and children, despite an extended ceasefire.

stories.title: Bangladesh: Albino buffalo nicknamed “Donald Trump” becomes unlikely star attraction
stories.summary: An albino buffalo nicknamed “Donald Trump” draws huge crowds near Dhaka ahead of Eid al-Adha, as visitors flock to see its rare blond coat.

stories.title: White House denies blocking Ebola evacuation
stories.summary: The White House has denied a report claiming that the United States blocked the return of Ebola-infected missionary doctor Peter Stafford.

stories.title: Jeanette revives cult film anthem "Por qué te vas" in Cannes event
stories.summary: Jeanette, the voice behind the iconic song Porque te vas from 1976’s Cría Cuervos, led celebrations in Cannes as France marked the film’s 50th anniversary with a restored screening and live performance at the Cannes Film Festival.

stories.title: Iran secretly executes four as it hits global death sentence record
stories.summary: Four more men, including two Kurdish political activists, were put to death in secret this week without informing their families, as Amnesty International recorded 2,159 executions in Iran last year — the highest of any country.

stories.title: Watch: 'Everybody back up!' Moment car explodes in NYC
stories.summary: Viral footage circulating online shows flames and thick black smoke enveloping the street in New York.

stories.title: Who is Raúl Castro: the man who inherited the revolution and still shapes Cuba under pressure
stories.summary: Fidel Castro’s brother and a central pillar of Cuban power for decades, the former leader is back in the spotlight after a US indictment, amid mounting pressure on the regime.

stories.title: Gaza students sit exams in tents as nearly all schools lie in ruins
stories.summary: With 658,000 children locked out of classrooms for two consecutive years, Gaza's official secondary exams begin next month. Teachers and students told Euronews what it means to prepare without textbooks, chairs — or walls.

stories.title: Pashinyan grabs megaphone as exchange turns heated during election campaign
stories.summary: Armenian's election campaign turns fiery as Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan grabbed a megaphone and confronted critics accusing him of giving up Karabakh, during an election campaign event in Yerevan.

stories.title: Ukrainian drone pilots compete in high-speed contests
stories.summary: Ukraine is turning FPV drone training into competitive tournaments to boost pilots’ speed, precision and tactics in its war against Russia.

stories.title: Intensifying heat wave grips northern India as temperatures near 48°C
stories.summary: India’s northern plains are enduring another intense heat wave, with authorities warning of prolonged extreme temperatures close to 45 degrees Celsius in New Delhi on Thursday. Cooling shelters and health alerts have been introduced across New Delhi and nearby states.

stories.title: Who is Ahmad Vahidi, Interpol-wanted IRGC general and key Iranian war strategist?
stories.summary: Ahmad Vahidi, deputy IRGC commander and the man Argentina formally accused of planning the 1994 Buenos Aires bombing that killed 85 people, has emerged as the key figure in organising Tehran's military response and partaking in back-channel negotiations with regional mediators.

stories.title: Iran steps up claim to control Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran publishes a map claiming "armed forces oversight" across more than 22,000 sq km of the waterway.

stories.title: 'Historic' UK trade deal with Gulf states set to add billions to British economy
stories.summary: The deal is the first of its kind between the GCC and a G7 country, and will scrap tariffs on billions of euros worth of British exports.

stories.title: Rubio says US focused on changing politics in Cuba as Havana accepts €86m in aid
stories.summary: Rubio, a Cuban-American and vociferous opponent of Havana's government, described the island 145 kilometres from the US shore as a "failed state" as it suffers a major economic crisis.

stories.title: Ancient Buddhist hall with 1,000-year-old ‘eternal flame’ burns in Japan
stories.summary: A Buddhist hall on Japan’s Miyajima Island that housed an “eternal flame” believed to have burned for more than 1,000 years was destroyed in a fire, with aerial footage showing the building engulfed in flames.
